Reduces probe frequency 6x to stay within B2 free Class C cap.\",\"source_repo\":\".\",\"compaction_level\":0,\"original_size\":0,\"labels\":[\"deployment\",\"external-repo\"],\"dependencies\":[{\"issue_id\":\"armor-hnq.3\",\"depends_on_id\":\"armor-hnq\",\"type\":\"parent-child\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-23T21:37:49.310485590Z\",\"created_by\":\"coding\",\"metadata\":\"{}\",\"thread_id\":\"\"}]}\n.beads/.br_history/issues.20260429_171251_834719261.jsonl:{\"id\":\"armor-it3\",\"title\":\"Suppress misleading WARN when B2 key is bucket-scoped\",\"description\":\"## Problem\\n\\nAt startup ARMOR logs:\\n\\n```\\nWARN  Failed to create B2 keys client - key management disabled\\n      error: b2_authorize_account: 401:\\n```\\n\\n...whenever the configured B2 application key is scoped to a single bucket (the production/recommended configuration). Bucket-scoped app keys authenticate correctly for S3 ops against their bucket but **cannot** call the account-level `b2_authorize_account` endpoint that the keys-management subsystem needs. The 401 is returned by B2 by design.\\n\\nThis has caused false-alarm investigations on both `ardenone-hub` (bucket `armor-apexalgo`) and `ord-devimprint` (bucket `devimprint`) — operators saw the WARN, assumed credentials were broken, and chased it before realizing S3 traffic was flowing fine.\\n\\n## Fix\\n\\nDetect the bucket-scoped case and either:\\n\\n1. **Preferred**: if `ARMOR_BUCKET` is set, skip the keys-client init entirely and log at INFO: `B2 key management disabled (application key is bucket-scoped)`.\\n2. **Minimum**: downgrade the log level to INFO and rewrite the message so it reads as expected behavior, not a failure. E.g.: `B2 key management unavailable — application key lacks account-level scope (this is normal when ARMOR_BUCKET is set)`.\\n\\nOption 1 avoids even making the failing API call; option 2 keeps current behavior but stops scaring operators.\\n\\n## Acceptance\\n\\n- Starting ARMOR with a bucket-scoped B2 key emits no WARN or ERROR about `b2_authorize_account`.\\n- S3 read/write paths still work identically.\\n- If someone misconfigures a truly invalid key (wrong id/secret), they still get a clear error at S3-op time.\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"priority\":2,\"issue_type\":\"bug\",\"assignee\":\"juliet\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-23T00:57:50.219577235Z\",\"created_by\":\"coding\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-23T01:01:05.874126407Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-23T01:01:05.873855851Z\",\"close_reason\":\"Fixed misleading WARN when B2 key is bucket-scoped. When ARMOR_BUCKET is set (production config), the B2 application key is scoped to a single bucket and cannot call account-level B2 API endpoints like b2_authorize_account. Previously, ARMOR would attempt to create the B2 keys client regardless, resulting in a 401 error logged as WARN. Now, when ARMOR_BUCKET is set, we skip the B2 keys client initialization entirely and log at INFO level. Clients that sign with a different region in their Credential scope fail with:\\n\\n```\\nAccessDenied: Invalid credentials\\n```\\n\\neven though their access key / secret pair is correct. This has already bitten at least one caller on `ord-devimprint`: the devimprint workers hardcode `region_name='auto'` in their boto3 client (an R2 convention), which silently overrides `AWS_DEFAULT_REGION`. Every PutObject against ARMOR was rejected on signature mismatch despite valid credentials.\\n\\nThe region check adds no real security or tenant-separation — ARMOR is a single-tenant proxy in front of a single B2 bucket. Region in the Credential scope is informational only; rejecting mismatches just turns ARMOR into a footgun for S3 clients written for R2, MinIO, or any non-AWS endpoint that historically ignores region.\\n\\n## Fix\\n\\nAccept any non-empty region string in the SigV4 Credential scope. The signature recomputation must still use whatever region the client claimed — otherwise the HMAC chain breaks — but ARMOR should **not** require the claimed region to match its own `ARMOR_B2_REGION` config.\\n\\nConcretely in the verifier:\\n\\n- Parse `Credential=<access_key>/<date>/<region>/s3/aws4_request`.\\n- Recompute the signing key using the parsed `<region>` (not the configured region).\\n- Compare the recomputed signature to the provided one.\\n- Accept if they match; reject only on real signature mismatch, missing/bad credential, or bad key ID.\\n\\n## Acceptance\\n\\n- Clients signing with `region='auto'`, `region='us-east-1'`, or `region='us-west-002'` all succeed when their access key / secret is valid.\\n- Clients with a wrong secret still get AccessDenied (signature mismatch).\\n- Clients with a bogus access key ID still get AccessDenied.\\n- Existing clients that correctly use `us-west-002` continue to work unchanged.\\n- Add a test covering at least `auto`, `us-east-1`, and the configured region, each with a valid and an invalid secret.\\n\\n## Why this over fixing clients\\n\\nThere's a parallel option of patching each caller (devimprint worker code, any future caller) to read region from env or hardcode the right value. That's a losing game — every new S3 library or tool that's R2- or MinIO-flavored will repeat this trap, and each failure surfaces as \\\"Invalid credentials\\\" which points investigators at secrets, not region config. Making ARMOR tolerant once fixes it forever.\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"priority\":2,\"issue_type\":\"feature\",\"assignee\":\"alpha\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-23T10:58:07.838621756Z\",\"created_by\":\"coding\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-23T11:13:23.510185299Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-23T11:13:23.509992294Z\",\"close_reason\":\"Loosened SigV4 region validation to accept any non-empty region in the Credential scope. buildStringToSign and getSigningKeyForCredential now take an explicit region parameter from the client's credential scope instead of using configured B2 region. Clients with region=auto, us-east-1, etc. now succeed with valid credentials; wrong secrets still rejected. Since commits/ objects are write-once parquet files, listing the same prefix twice within a short window returns identical results. ARMOR forwards every list request directly to B2 — no deduplication or caching.\\n\\nAt peak (both aggregator + website-builder every 5 min, 30-day DuckDB partition scan): ~17,000 Class C calls/day, exhausting B2 transaction caps.\\n\\n## Solution\\nAdd a TTL cache for ListObjectsV2 responses in ARMOR. Cache key: bucket + prefix + delimiter + maxKeys + continuationToken. TTL: configurable via ARMOR_LIST_CACHE_TTL env var, default 60s.\\n\\nCommits/ objects are immutable after write, so a 60s TTL is safe — clients will see new objects within one TTL window.\\n\\n## Scope\\n- New ListCache type in internal/backend/cache.go\\n- Cache integration in ListObjectsV2 handler (handlers.go)\\n- Cache invalidation on PutObject/DeleteObject for the affected prefix\\n- ARMOR_LIST_CACHE_TTL env var (default 60, 0 = disabled)\\n- ARMOR_LIST_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES env var (default 1000)\\n- Tests\\n- Deployment env var in declarative-config armor-deployment.yml\",\"status\":\"closed\",\"priority\":1,\"issue_type\":\"feature\",\"assignee\":\"armor-fix\",\"created_at\":\"2026-04-24T12:31:24.379121349Z\",\"created_by\":\"coding\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-27T01:21:08.792724272Z\",\"closed_at\":\"2026-04-27T01:21:08.792613372Z\",\"close_reason\":\"Summary of what was done:\\n\\nThe ListObjectsV2 response cache feature was already fully implemented in the ARMOR codebase:\\n\\n**Code (already implemented):**\\n- internal/backend/cache.go: ListCache type with Get/Set/InvalidatePrefix methods\\n- internal/config/config.go: ARMOR_LIST_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES (1000) and ARMOR_LIST_CACHE_TTL (60) env vars\\n- internal/server/server.go: ListCache creation and wiring to handlers\\n- internal/server/handlers/handlers.go: Cache integration in ListObjectsV2 handler and invalidation on PutObject/DeleteObject\\n- internal/backend/cache_test.go: Comprehensive tests (all passing)\\n\\n**Changes made:**\\n- Added explicit ARMOR_LIST_CACHE_MAX_ENTRIES and ARMOR_LIST_CACHE_TTL env vars to deployment configs:\\n  - error\n\tShouldRecord(key string) bool\n}\n\n// ManifestEntry holds the decryption metadata for a tracked object as exposed\n// to handlers. It mirrors manifest.Entry but is defined here to avoid an\n// import cycle between the handlers and manifest packages.\ntype ManifestEntry struct {\n\tPlaintextSize int64\n\tContentType   string\n\tETag          string\n\tLastModified  time.Time\n\tIV            []byte\n\tWrappedDEK    []byte\n\tBlockSize     int\n}\n\n// ManifestRecorder records successful S3 write operations in the manifest\n// index for fast in-memory lookup and enqueues them for async B2 persistence\n// as delta files. Implementations must be safe for concurrent use.\ntype ManifestRecorder interface {\n\t// RecordPut records a successful PutObject or CompleteMultipartUpload.\n\tRecordPut(bucket, key string, size int64, sha256Hex string, iv, wrappedDEK []byte, blockSize int, contentType, etag string)\n\t// RecordDelete records a successful DeleteObject.\n\tRecordDelete(bucket, key string)\n\t// Lookup returns manifest metadata for bucket/key, or (nil, false) if not\n\t// tracked. Used to serve HeadObject and ListObjectVersions from memory\n\t// without a B2 round-trip.\n\tLookup(bucket, key string) (*ManifestEntry, bool)\n}\n\n// Handlers contains all S3 operation handlers.\ntype Handlers struct {\n\tconfig      *config.Config\n\tbackend     backend.Backend\n\tcache       *backend.MetadataCache\n\tfooterCache *backend.FooterCache\n\tlistCache   *backend.ListCache\n\tkeyManager  *keymanager.KeyManager\n\tprovenance  ProvenanceRecorder\n\tmanifest    ManifestRecorder\n}\n\n// New creates a new Handlers instance.\nfunc New(cfg *config.Config, be backend.Backend, cache *backend.MetadataCache, footerCache *backend.FooterCache, km *keymanager.KeyManager, listCache *backend.ListCache) *Handlers {\n\treturn &Handlers{\n\t\tconfig:      cfg,\n\t\tbackend:     be,\n\t\tcache:       cache,\n\t\tfooterCache: footerCache,\n\t\tlistCache:   listCache,\n\t\tkeyManager:  km,\n\t\tprovenance:  nil,\n\t\tmanifest:    nil,\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithProvenance adds provenance support to handlers.\nfunc (h *Handlers) WithProvenance(p ProvenanceRecorder) {\n\th.provenance = p\n}\n\n// WithManifest wires a ManifestRecorder into the handlers so that successful\n// PutObject and DeleteObject calls update the in-memory index and enqueue\n// delta ops for async B2 persistence.\nfunc (h *Handlers) WithManifest(m ManifestRecorder) {\n\th.manifest = m\n}","numLines":100,"startLine":1,"totalLines":2529}}}
